nim_nii.summary: NII rose 5% year-over-year to $12.3B and 2% sequentially to $12.3B, driven by lower deposit costs, higher loan and investment securities balances, and balance sheet growth in Markets. NIM compressed to 2.43% from 2.68% a year ago and 2.47% last quarter, reflecting the drag from lower rates on floating-rate assets. No explicit full-year NII guidance range was stated in the release.
nim_nii.quote: Net interest income increased 5%, driven by lower deposit costs, higher loan and investment securities balances, the impact of lower interest rates and balance sheet growth in our Markets business, and higher interest-bearing commercial deposits, partially offset by the impact of lower interest rates on floating rate assets, as well as a modest decline in noninterest-bearing deposits
nim_nii.speaker: Earnings Release (Selected Company-wide Financial Information)

credit_normalization.summary: Credit is clearly improving: the NCO rate fell to 0.34% annualized in Q2 2026 from 0.45% in Q1 2026 and 0.44% a year ago. Nonperforming assets dropped $824M quarter-over-quarter. Both consumer and commercial charge-off rates declined, led by lower auto, credit card, and C&I NCOs. Management described consumer and commercial credit quality as 'strong across all portfolios.'
credit_normalization.quote: Consumer and commercial credit quality remained strong across all portfolios and net loan charge-offs declined 10 basis points from a year ago.
credit_normalization.speaker: Charlie Scharf, Chairman and CEO

cre_reserves.summary: Wells Fargo released CRE allowance for credit losses this quarter. The overall allowance increased modestly, driven by higher reserves for credit card and auto loans due to higher balances, but this was 'largely offset by a lower allowance for commercial real estate loans.' CRE nonaccrual loans also declined quarter-over-quarter, contributing to the reserve release.
cre_reserves.quote: Provision for credit losses in second quarter 2026 included a modest increase in the allowance for credit losses, reflecting a higher allowance for credit card and auto loans driven by higher loan balances, largely offset by a lower allowance for commercial real estate loans
cre_reserves.speaker: Earnings Release (Selected Company-wide Financial Information)

deposit_costs.summary: Deposit costs are falling and acting as a tailwind to NII. Lower deposit pricing was cited as a primary driver of NII growth year-over-year in multiple segments, including Wealth and Investment Management where NII rose 17% driven explicitly by 'lower deposit pricing.' The release does not provide a specific deposit cost rate or beta figure.
deposit_costs.quote: Net interest income was up 17% driven by lower deposit pricing and higher deposit and loan balances
deposit_costs.speaker: Earnings Release (Wealth and Investment Management segment)

capital_return.summary: Wells Fargo repurchased $3.0B (37.4M shares) in Q2 2026 and ~$7B in H1 2026. The Q3 2026 dividend is expected to increase 11% to $0.50/share. CET1 held steady at 10.3% quarter-over-quarter (down from 11.1% a year ago). Management noted the stress capital buffer remains at the 2.5% floor and expressed confidence in capital generation, flagging pending finalization of new proposed capital rules.
capital_return.quote: During the first half of this year, we repurchased approximately $7 billion of common stock and as previously announced, we expect to increase our third quarter common stock dividend by 11% to $0.50 per common share, subject to approval by the Company's Board of Directors at its meeting later this month. We continue to manage our capital position with significant buffers above regulatory minimums and look forward to the finalization of the new proposed capital rules
capital_return.speaker: Charlie Scharf, Chairman and CEO

loan_growth.summary: Average loans grew 12% year-over-year and 3% quarter-over-quarter to $1,026.5B — all organic, with no acquisitions mentioned as a driver. Growth was broad-based: CIB loans up 26% YoY (aided by Markets balance sheet growth), Commercial Banking up 5%, Consumer up 6%, and WIM up 12%. Management noted deliberate, selective deployment of capital now that the asset cap is no longer a constraint.
loan_growth.quote: Our balance sheet continued to grow with average loans increasing 12% from a year ago, with higher balances in both our consumer and commercial businesses, and average deposits increasing 10%.
loan_growth.speaker: Charlie Scharf, Chairman and CEO

macro_outlook.summary: Management is constructive on the near-term environment but deliberately cautious about extrapolating it forward. Scharf cited broad-based economic strength, strong labor markets, all-time-high equity indices, and robust consumer and business balance sheets, while also flagging affordability/inflation concerns and signaling selective growth discipline given the inevitability of future shocks.
macro_outlook.quote: Consumers and businesses remain very strong. Consumer spending is higher, charge-offs and delinquencies are lower, and savings and investments are growing across consumer segments. Businesses are cautious but balance sheets and cash flows remain strong resulting in strong credit performance. Equity indices are at or near all-time highs, credit spreads are narrow, and there is a significant amount of liquidity being deployed by banks and non-banks. Concerns around affordability and inflation exist, but the labor market and wage growth remain strong. We know that such favorable conditions do not go on forever so we are being selective about how much and where to grow.
macro_outlook.speaker: Charlie Scharf, Chairman and CEO

notable_qa.summary: No live Q&A transcript was available — the source document is the written earnings release (8-K Ex. 99.1), not the earnings call transcript. Therefore no analyst Q&A exchanges can be reported.

source: Q2 2026 earnings release (8-K EX-99.1, EDGAR) -- call transcript not freely published as of 2026-07-21; quotes are from the written release
